message.reasoning_content:
We are at stage web-005-nested-shared-table. The goal: Create webpage 5 and add a shared nested table or shared data section that all five pages use consistently. We have existing files: site/index.html, site/page-2.html, site/page-3.html, site/page-4.html (from previous stage). They currently have shared navigation (as per stage 4). We need to create page-5.html and add a shared nested table or shared data section that all five pages use consistently. We must not break existing validators: shared-table-all-pages (failure_class=shared_state) and all-pages-linked (failure_class=link_integrity). The shared-table-all-pages likely checks that there is a shared table (or data section) present in all pages. The all-pages-linked likely checks that all pages are linked together (maybe via the navigation?). We already have shared navigation across all pages (from stage 4). However, the validator for this stage is shared-table-all-pages, not shared-nav-all-pages. So we need to add a shared nested table or shared data section. We can add a table (maybe with some data) that is identical across all pages. We should also create page-5.html with the same shared table and navigation. We must update all five pages (index.html, page-2.html, page-3.html, page-4.html, page-5.html) to include the shared table. We should also ensure that the navigation includes page-5.html (since we are adding a new page). The shared navigation from stage 4 only included pages 1-4. Now we need to update the navigation to include page-5 as well. However, note that the validator for this stage is shared-table-all-pages and all-pages-linked. The all-pages-linked validator might check that all pages are linked (maybe via the navigation). So we should update the navigation to include page-5. But we must be careful: the existing files from stage 4 already have navigation with links to pages 1-4. We are now at stage 5, so we need to modify those files again to add page-5 to the navigation and also add the shared table. Thus we need to modify: - site/index.html - site/page-2.html - site/page-3.html - site/page-4.html - and create site/page-5.html All five pages should have: 1. Updated navigation that includes links to all five pages (index.html, page-2.html, page-3.html, page-4.html, page-5.html). 2. A shared nested table (or data section) that is identical across all pages. We can place the table maybe after the navigation or inside the main content? The existing main content has some text and a back link. We should preserve the existing main content (to not break existing validators? The validators for previous stages might have checked for specific links in main content. However, the validators for this stage are only shared-table-all-pages and all-pages-linked. The previous validators are not re-run? In a staged benchmark, each stage's validators are run after that stage. So we must ensure that the current stage's validators pass, but we don't need to worry about previous validators? Actually, the system likely runs all validators up to the current stage? The description: "Validators that will run:" for this stage. It doesn't say they re-run previous validators. However, to be safe, we should not break existing links that were required by previous validators (like page-2-links-index, page-3-links-page-2, page-4-links-page-3). Those validators checked for specific links in the main content. We should preserve those links. Thus we will keep the main content as it was (with the welcome text and the back link) and add the shared table perhaps after the main content or before? We can put it in a separate section, maybe after the main content but still inside body. Alternatively, we could replace the main content with the table? That would break the existing validators.
